codeReview.summary: The fix commit correctly addresses four prior blocking findings (atomic save lock scope, '..' substring sanitization, strict final-review args parsing, and uninstall coverage matrix). However, the completion-gate fix remains insufficient: the VAL-CORRECT-031 test still does not uniquely exercise the requireFinalReview guard.

issues.description: The "required final review not passing" case now omits finalReview and expects missing_final_review_payload (/flow-status), which is already covered by the prior "missing final review on final feature" case (lines 238-246). In guard order, `wasFinalFeature && !worker.finalReview` (src/runtime/transitions/execution-completion-guards.ts:107) fails before the requireFinalReview-specific check (line 113), so removing the dedicated requireFinalReview branch would still leave this test green. This does not resolve the original isolation failure and also diverges from VAL-CORRECT-031 contract expectations (`validation-contract.md`:252-253, failing_final_review with reset command).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Contract and test expectations for VAL-CORRECT-031 are now out of sync, which makes future scrutiny ambiguous.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission contract still states VAL-CORRECT-031 should produce failing_final_review + reset command (`/Users/vriesd/.factory/missions/34ec2e58-6360-41eb-aaf2-3aba539ddf02/validation-contract.md`:252-253), while the new matrix case expects missing_final_review_payload + /flow-status (`tests/completion-gates.test.ts`:249-261).

summary: Fail. Four of five fix areas are adequately addressed, but the completion-gate isolation fix is still blocking because the VAL-CORRECT-031 case remains non-isolated and contract-misaligned.
